content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Limit collection, use, retention, access, export, and deletion of follows, read state, searches, content warnings, and device metadata.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Reader Data Minimization model · Theory Feed Ts…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Reader Data Minimization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Reader Data Minimization model · Theory Feed Ts…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 3204 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove token verification, resource policy, tenant repositories, data minimization, and audit redaction; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Limit collection, use, retention, access, export, and deletion of follows, read state, searches, content warnings, and device metadata.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Reader Data Minimization model · Theory Feed Ts…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Personalization data is operational and therefore nonsensitive.
